svg.font-family: ui-monospace, SF Mono, Menlo, Consolas, monospace
svg.aria-label: Two encoding paths converge in one latent lake: client encoders run on the spacecraft and downlink latents; on the ground, the open planet is encoded in-tenant by a sidecar zoo of foundation models (Prithvi, Galileo, Clay, JEPA-v2). The lake is sensor-agnostic. Decoders consume both jointly and write signed, content-addressed facts to a cache that AI agents read.
svg.title: your encoder in orbit · the free planet encoded on ground · decoded together
svg.desc: Path A: each customer satellite carries the customer's proprietary encoder on the bus; only the latent (~1 KB) downlinks; weights stay on the spacecraft and the customer's tenant. Path B: emem pulls 40+ open data sources (Sentinel-1/-2, Landsat, MODIS, Cop-DEM, ESA WorldCover, NASA POWER, ERA5, FIRMS, Hansen, JRC GSW, SoilGrids, GMRT, WorldPop, Overture, GeoNames, FTW, ...) and encodes them in-tenant with a GPU-pinned sidecar zoo of foundation encoders (Prithvi-EO-2.0-300M-TL, Galileo, Clay v1.5, JEPA-v2, plus a plug-in slot). Both paths write to the same parquet shape keyed (cell64, tslot, model_id, sensor_id). Decoders — linear probes (supporting fusion across encoders), reconstruction heads, archetype matchers, cross-modal alignment — read from the lake and emit canonical CBOR facts, BLAKE3-addressed and ed25519-signed, into the sled-backed cache. Agents read facts via /v1/recall, /v1/find_similar, /v1/trajectory.

g.text: your encoder in orbit · the free planet encoded on ground · decoded together
weights stay where you trust them. latents meet in the lake. agents read signed facts.
PATH A · ORBIT · client encoders on the bus
PATH B · GROUND · open planet encoded in your tenant
your weights never left the spacecraft. only the latent did.
all anonymous · no API keys · pulled by emem-fetch
+ ~20 more streams in sources-v0.json · STAC search · COG range reads · parquet row-group prune
sidecar · GPU encoder zoo · in your tenant
FastAPI · /run/emem/jepa_sidecar.sock · CBOR · pinned · dispatched by sensor type
parquet shards on S3 · GCS · Azure · keys: (cell64, tslot, model_id, sensor_id) · orbit-origin and ground-origin records have the same shape · the lake is sensor-agnostic
DECODERS · consume any latent · fuse across encoders
private signal × public memory → better per-cell answer
pixels never re-downloaded — recovered from latent
no training · no weights · just memory
linear bridge · ridge-fit on overlapping cells
your latents talk to ours, and ours to yours
signed-fact cache · the surface AI agents read
(cell, band, tslot) → fact_cid · canonical CBOR · BLAKE3 content-address · ed25519 signature · sled
